entry.title: The Chinese Adventure 2014: Attraction & Travel Tickets
entry.summary: Attraction & Travel Tickets from the Chinese Adventure.
entry.content: Attraction & Travel Tickets from the Chinese Adventure.

entry.title: The Chinese Adventure 2014: Hong Kong – Part Two
entry.summary: The view from Victoria Peak in Hong Kong is famous worldwide, it’s…
entry.content: The view from Victoria Peak in Hong Kong is famous worldwide, it’s just one of those things you can’t miss when you come over here, and you’re need a pretty decent camera to get the right night time shot (I didn’t quite get what I was hoping for here but I’m still happy with the result). On my second to last day I took the high speed catamaran to the island of Macau, this place is like a mini Las Vegas! Plenty of cool camera opportunities here, crazy majestic buildings, sounds and lights, was a great way to finish what has been an awesome trip! China ~ the size, the culture, the history! ~ this is a truly remarkable country that looks up to no other country, it stands alone in the world, what a great privilege it has been to visit it.

entry.title: The Chinese Adventure 2014: Hong Kong – Part One
entry.summary: After a long 12 hour coach ride from Guilin, getting through passport control…
entry.content: After a long 12 hour coach ride from Guilin, getting through passport control in Shenzhen, I was finally on the last metro train to Hong Kong, got to my hotel in Kowloon and hit the sack. Next day I visited the Central Business District (CBD) and was greeted by the sea of protesters tents covering the streets, things had almost died down here but the protesters weren’t giving up that easily. Luckly for me this gave an interesting spin on the photography side of things so I got snapping away and checked out what was happening then walked up to Causeway Bay to catch the classic tram. Next few days was full of checking out this amazing city & also a trip to see the Tian Tan Buddha, serious bit of bronze statue! and at night I watched the Symphony of Lights show on the Victoria Harbour.

entry.title: The Chinese Adventure 2014: Yangshuo County
entry.summary: Flying out of Chengdu and now arriving into the beautiful countryside of Yangshuo…
entry.content: Flying out of Chengdu and now arriving into the beautiful countryside of Yangshuo County, Southern China and you get the feel for how big this country is! different terrain, climate, attitudes etc. The relaxed pace of things here was a welcome break and the Phoenix Pagoda Fonglou Retreat is one of the best accommodations I’ve ever stayed in, it was more like living in the home of a Chinese family, Lily and the all the staff were amazing ~ if you ever visit the Yangshuo area I couldn’t recommend this place enough! The main reason I wanted to visit here was actually a photo I saw on the web from one of my favourite photographers, Trey Ratcliff, it was of Laozhai Mountain and I knew I just had to see this amazing place!

entry.title: The Chinese Adventure 2014: Chengdu – Part Two
entry.summary: One of my other reasons for including Chengdu on my trip (apart…
entry.content:
One of my other reasons for including Chengdu on my trip (apart from the Giant Buddha) was to visit the Chengdu Panda Breeding & Research Center, organising a trip with the hotel I got there early one morning to catch the pandas meal times, I still remember seeing the very first one amongst all the bamboo trees sitting there like a baby and chewing away on their favourite pastime, just like the one in the photo, brilliant. The rest of my time in Chengdu I spent wandering around the big city and seeing what interesting stuff I could find, I noticed the ‘new found wealth’ China creeping in with old buildings being knocked down and modern ones being put up…

Chengdu Panda Breeding & Research Center Photos

 The People's Park Photos

Tianfu Square & General Chengdu Photos

 Jinli Pedestrian Street Photos

entry.title: The Chinese Adventure 2014: Chengdu – Part One
entry.summary: The Wenjun Mansion, the hotel I was staying in, and in fact…
entry.content:
The Wenjun Mansion, the hotel I was staying in, and in fact all the other buildings in the same street were spectacular structures to see and appreciate coming from an ancient time, this theme also carried on with the Kuanxiangzi Alley just around the corner which dates back to the Qing Dynasty (1644 – 1911 AD).

The Wenjun Mansion Photos

Kuanxiangzi Alley Photos

General Chengdu Photos

entry.title: The Chinese Adventure 2014: Leshan
entry.summary: Upon arriving at my accommodation in Chengdu, I enquired about the weather…
entry.content:
Upon arriving at my accommodation in Chengdu, I enquired about the weather conditions as it was pretty smoggy outside and I wanted to see the Leshan Giant Buddha on the clearest day I had here, at reception they told me that this was gonna be the best day so without even checking in I was off on a two hour coach ride to Leshan to see this extraordinary sight that I’ve dreamt about for the last 6 months ~ and after seeing the Buddha in all his glory I was not disappointed! 

Leshan Giant Buddha Photos

entry.title: The Chinese Adventure 2014: Jiuzhaigou and Huanglong
entry.summary: Flying into the heart of China, Jiuzhaigou, was breathtaking! (and cold), leaving…
entry.content:
Flying into the heart of China, Jiuzhaigou, was breathtaking! (and cold), leaving the big cities behind and seeing the rocky snow-capped mountains upon arrival I felt like I was in an entirely different place altogether! I had two days here the first day I visited the snow-clad mountains of Huanglong Valley and trekked all the way up to the famous Multi-colored Pond, after coming back down from the mountains I couldn’t find my taxi driver, the nice Malaysian people I was sharing the ride with and my luggage! about an hour later and all was sorted (was starting to get a bit worried for a bit until the drivers mysteriously turned up and Malaysian group started to appear out of the exit) and then it was a gruelling 3/4 hour taxi journey late into the night to the mega busy Jiuzhaigou, and more trouble with my accommodation and nowhere accepting visa cards but that’s another story… next day was spent in what can only be described as one of the most scenic places I’ve ever been to in my life “fullstop”. Needless to say I let the photo’s do the talking from this point…

Huanglong Valley

Jiuzhaigou Photos

entry.title: The Chinese Adventure 2014: Xi’an
entry.summary: After my stay in Pingyao I caught another bullet train to Xi’an,…
entry.content:
After my stay in Pingyao I caught another bullet train to Xi’an, home of The Terracotta Army! I’ve seen this place on TV quite a few times but nothing can prepare you for the first time you see it in real life ~ just wow, the size of this place is incredible, as you can see in the photo it’s of stadium proportions. I also hired a bike and rode along the City Wall of Xian, got some good snaps when dusk fell too!

General Xi'an Photos

The Terracotta Army Photos

City Wall of Xian Photos

entry.title: The Chinese Adventure 2014: Pingyao
entry.summary: After a bit of trouble with the trains (not knowing Beijing had…
entry.content:
After a bit of trouble with the trains (not knowing Beijing had no less than four main railway stations, I went to catch my bullet train to Pingyao from the wrong one and consequently missed it, luckily not only did I get a full refund but also the next train a few hours later on that same day – phew…) I arrived in smoggy Pingyao! This really felt like old China (this city dates back some 2,700 years) and staying in a quaint hotel in the middle of the city was surreal, I don’t think I saw even one westerner at this place! I took a walk along the City walls & checked out banking buildings from the past ~ Pingyao was the financial centre of China back in the day. On my leaving I also took a trip to Shuanglin Temple to see The thousand-armed Guanyin!

Pingyao Photos

Shuanglin Temple Photos
